reviews.review_description:
Hi everyone, I’d like to say that the concept of the game is fantastic. The gameplay and attention to detail are truly impressive. I understand that incorporating AI may not be part of the original design; however, in practice, it can be difficult to consistently find other players, even through Discord. Since some modes require multiple participants, adding AI could significantly improve accessibility and allow players to choose between solo and multiplayer experiences.

On top of that, you could potentially increase revenue by doing so, as it would encourage people to buy the game even if they can’t always find other players.
For instance, right now I’m very keen to play, but my wife is working and there isn’t anyone available. It kind of sucks because most of the game modes require more than one player.
A simple addition like this could make the game more profitable and more enjoyable for everyone.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the kind words! Really glad All On Board! is helping you spend time with friends. On the AI players question, the platform works just like a real tabletop, meaning players handle the rules and gameplay themselves, so there are no AI opponents for now. That said, good to know it's something you'd like to see! More games on the way, so stay tuned. Thanks for the support!

reviews.review_description:
I was looking for a way to play boardgames with friends overseas (I’m in NA, while most of my boardgames group is based in Europe). 

This is a great simulator for that. We’ve had three sessions so far and each one has been a great experience. The only thing I’d say is that I would love to have more games to choose from (a bigger catalog). Other than that, it’s fun, and very immersive. 

Given the price, I do think I got the best bang for my buck.

developer_response.body: Thank you for sharing this! It genuinely made us smile knowing All On Board! is helping you and your crew bridge that NA–Europe distance. We're working on expanding the catalog, so there'll be more to explore soon. Hope the next sessions are just as fun as the first three. See you on board!

reviews.review_description: I did not expect this to be so much fun. It actually felt like if I was playing IRL with my friends, without the hassle of having to set up and put away the game afterwards. Also, games are really accessible thanks to the new platform features, so everything feels much more dynamic. 10/10

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the review! Really glad the experience feels close to playing IRL and that the new platform features are making games more accessible for you. We keep pushing updates to make it better with every release. See you on board!

reviews.review_title: Awesome way to play board games in VR!
reviews.review_description: As a huge board game fan, I should know that getting a group together locally can be tough. This game allowed me an alternative way to play board games. I can finally play with my friends living abroad, and it works great. The game selection is a bit limited right now and the community is still growing, but honestly, if they keep updating the catalog, I can't recommend it enough.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the kind words! Keeping friends connected across distances is exactly what we're going for, so it's great to hear it's working for you. More games are on the way, so stay tuned!

reviews.review_description:
When it comes to board games, I’m an old school veteran.. I’ve been dragging meeples around since the days of Basari, Puerto Rico or Power Grid.
Playing in VR!? I’ll just say I was pretty sceptical. Not to mention the motion sickness I’d experienced with this kind of devices before.
Even so, I gave it a go. We’ve now played several sessions of Istanbul, Terraforming Mars and the newcomer Hamlet, and I have to say it’s turned out to be a brilliant alternative. I prefer the XR mode, as the experience feels very natural (and, by the way, it doesn’t make me feel dizzy at all).
On the other hand, being able to save and restore a game’s previous state (without having to leave the game set up on the table) is something you can only do here.
That said, if I had to ask for more, it would be great to have a wider range of games and access to materials so we could test some proto we put together with friends (10/10 if that ever happened).

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review! Really glad All On Board! won you over, especially coming from such a long board gaming background. Great to know XR mode has felt comfortable and that saving and restoring games is making a difference. We've taken note of your interest in more games and custom content support. See you on board!

reviews.review_title: Real board games in VR, not a video game
reviews.review_description:
WARNING: this is not an automated board game with AI running everything for you. You’re expected to learn the rules and play the games yourself, just like at a physical table. If you’re looking for heavy automation or very guided gameplay, this probably isn’t for you.
 
All On Board! is a great way to play board games in VR if what you want is a true tabletop experience. Handling cards, dice, and pieces feels natural once you get used to it, and it really captures the feeling of sitting around a real table.
 
That said, learning new games isn’t necessarily easier than in real life. The rulebook is available in game and is helpful, but it’s the original one designed for the physical version, so it doesn’t always line up perfectly with the VR adaptation. In those cases, you may need to rely on the video tutorials. They can feel a bit long at first, but they explain everything clearly. I used them while learning Terraforming Mars in solo mode, and once things clicked, the experience became very enjoyable. You can tell the developers have put a lot of effort into helping players learn the games, but at the end of the day, you still need to put in a bit of effort yourself.
 
The game catalog is currently limited but clearly growing, with new games released gradually. The quality makes the wait worth it. The games are comfortable to play in VR, the environments are well done, and DLC prices feel reasonable, cheaper than buying the physical board games. While it’s not exactly the same as playing in person around a real table, it works very well as an alternative for playing online with friends. I’ve been playing on Meta Quest 3, and the XR mode feels especially good for longer sessions.
 
I’m giving it 5 stars because you can really feel the care and effort behind it. Even in its current state, it already offers a solid and genuinely enjoyable experience that keeps getting better over time. Highly recommended.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for taking the time to write such a thoughtful and detailed review. We truly appreciate how clearly you described what kind of experience All On Board! is, and just as importantly, what it is not. You’re absolutely right that the platform is built around recreating the feeling of a real tabletop, where players learn the rules and manage the game themselves, and we know that this can take a bit of effort at first. That’s why it means a lot to hear that the in-game rulebooks and video tutorials helped you through that learning curve, especially with a game like Terraforming Mars, as making learning more approachable without automating gameplay is something we continue to refine. We’re steadily expanding the catalog and sharing updates on our Discord roadmap (https://discord.gg/EumRYpZ3YW), always with a strong focus on quality. Thanks again for the 5-star rating and for recognizing the care and effort behind the project, feedback like yours genuinely motivates the team to keep improving All On Board!

reviews.review_description: This game is absolutely dead. Even in the official Discord you'll find nobody to play with. People turned away from the game because devs promised a lot and held very little. This is especially true for mod support which was one of the big things promised already before the release, now its not even on the roadmap anymore. Stay away from the game! It's sad but true.

developer_response.body: Thank you for taking the time to share your perspective. We are sorry to hear that your experience with All On Board! left you feeling this way, and we understand how frustrating it can be when expectations are not met. All On Board! is a virtual tabletop tool designed around intentional, social play, and activity levels can vary depending on time zones and when players are looking for games. Many players enjoy the platform most when playing with people they already know, but our Discord is also the main hub for finding other players, with LFG channels in multiple languages and additional tools to help organize and join games, which we explain here: https://discord.com/channels/969135533173383179/1402674257099489411 and through our Telegram community as well. Regarding mod support, it has not been cancelled and is still in development, but it is a complex system that requires time to build properly. We shared a detailed status update in our latest dev diary from early November 2025 (https://youtu.be/cTXSZzUrDNM?si=72OlIWaxy6UsV3mp), and we continue to post transparent updates through our Discord announcements channel (https://discord.gg/9xdX3rYr3n), always aiming to communicate clearly and avoid overpromising before features are ready. We genuinely appreciate feedback like this, even when it is critical, as it helps us better understand concerns and improve how All On Board! evolves.

reviews.review_title: Why did it take me so long to buy it?
reviews.review_description:
I came across this board game simulator last Christmas, and to be honest, I don't know why I took so long to buy it.

I’ve had one of the best times in VR playing with my friends. It’s an incredible way to put my Quest 3 to use. Each of us was playing from our own homes, and it totally felt like we were all gathered around the same table.

One of the things we’ve loved is the chance to try out games before buying them (Escape the Dark Castle is a blast!!!!)

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review! Really glad All On Board! was worth the wait! That feeling of being gathered around the same table even from different homes is exactly what we're going for. Happy to hear Escape the Dark Castle was a hit too! See you on board!

reviews.review_title: A board fans dream come true
reviews.review_description: Bought this at launch and it gets better with each update. Binding of Isaac is a really decent pack on this, and also Escape The Dark Castle (a game developed in little old Norfolk, UK). The multiplayer works flawlessly and although it can take a little time to learn the rules of each game - exactly like real life - it's so fun when you get going! There are more packs to come too, would love to play Skull in this

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the kind words and for supporting All On Board! since launch. It truly means a lot to us to hear that you’re enjoying the platform and noticing how it keeps improving with each update. You’re absolutely right, learning the rules takes a bit of time, just like sitting down at a real table, and that’s a core part of the experience we want to recreate. Hearing that everything clicks and becomes truly fun, especially in multiplayer, is incredibly rewarding for the team. We’re continuing to work on new content and improvements, and we regularly share updates on our Discord roadmap. If you have ideas like Skull or any other games you’d love to see, feel free to share them there. We genuinely love reading what players are excited about. Thanks again for taking the time to leave a review and for being part of All On Board!

reviews.review_title: Solid option to chill with friends
reviews.review_description: I’d definitely recommend this game if you want to play boardgames with friends that you can't meet in person. The tabletop experience is immersive and it feels like we are all gathered in the same room. I had lots of fun playing Terraforming Mars and we might try Istanbul next.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review! Really glad All On Board! is helping you connect and play with friends from a distance. Great to hear Terraforming Mars has been a hit, hope Istanbul is just as fun. See you on board!

reviews.review_title: Exactly what I was looking for!
reviews.review_description: Easy 5 stars. I love this game, it's super intuitive and just really fun to jump into. You can tell the devs put a ton of effort into every DLC too, the quality is consistently high and it really shows. Definitely one of my favorite VR experiences right now.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the review! Really glad All On Board! has been what you were looking for and that jumping in feels intuitive. Means a lot to hear you've noticed the care put into each DLC. See you on board!

reviews.review_description: So I finally did purchase the all in one bundle for this because I like playing board games and don't really have anyone else to play with within the family. The only game I knew the rules of in this was the "escape the dark castle" and I did enjoy playing this (solo as well as multiplayer). However, while playing solo and taking turns for multiple players, I really did think that this could have done with having AI players. I am looking forward to learning the rules for the other board games and enjoying them like the only other game that I do understand. If the developers are reading this then please... PLEASE this awesome game could really use a solo AI mode for players who would like to practice and learn the rules. If you still are on the fence about buying this game and you would like to re-live your youth by playing board games with other people: I would suggest you buy it (even the base game as you do not need to purchase the games as long as the other player has them). It has a nice selection of games and hopefully, more to come.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your review and for giving the bundle a try. It’s great to hear that you enjoyed Escape the Dark Castle, both solo and in multiplayer, and that you’re excited to dive into the rest of the games.

About your suggestion regarding AI-controlled players, we understand why that could be helpful for practicing or learning a game more comfortably. All On Board! is built as a virtual tabletop platform, so the focus is on recreating the feeling of playing at a physical table rather than automating rules or adding computer-controlled opponents. Even so, feedback like yours gives us valuable insight into how players would like to use the platform, and it’s something we’ll keep in mind as we continue refining the experience.

Thanks again for your kind words and for recommending All On Board! We hope you have a great time exploring the rest of the games, and we truly appreciate your support as the platform keeps growing.

reviews.review_description:
This product surprised me very positively, I’ve been hanging out with my friends to play Istanbul and Escape The Dark Castle using this and honestly, we all ended up feeling like we were playing around a real table.

The games are already set up and good to go when you start the session so zero hassle there, and the controls are super intuitive. We basically logged in and focused on learning the rules and playing right away. Great find!

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the review! Really glad All On Board! brought back that feeling of sitting around a real table with friends, that's exactly what we're going for. Great to hear Istanbul and Escape The Dark Castle felt smooth to jump into as well. See you on board!

reviews.review_title: 🎲 The Ultimate VR Board Game Hub
reviews.review_description:
All On Board! is a fantastic VR board game experience built around a sophisticated and beautifully presented game table. The interface is sleek, and the tutorial is clear and well designed, making it easy for new players to quickly navigate and feel comfortable.

Immersion is strong, with a solid and growing variety of board games to choose from. A standout feature is how the developers support learning: direct YouTube links for visual explanations alongside written materials ensure every type of player can understand and enjoy a new game with ease. The studio’s commitment to regular updates and new content is also very reassuring.

I’m especially looking forward to diving into multiplayer sessions. A rock-solid VR board game platform that truly has something for everyone.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the thoughtful review and for highlighting so many aspects of All On Board! that are important to us. We’re really glad to hear that the interface, tutorial and overall presentation helped you feel comfortable so quickly, especially as a new player. Supporting learning is a big focus for the platform, so it means a lot to know that the mix of written rules and video resources is making it easier and more approachable to jump into new games. Multiplayer is where All On Board! truly comes to life, and we hope you have a great time once you start playing with others. We regularly share updates and upcoming plans on our Discord roadmap, so feel free to join the conversation there. Thanks again for taking the time to share your experience and for supporting the platform.

reviews.review_description: Im backer!
Please release the Brass: Birmingham board game as well.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review and for the suggestion. Brass: Birmingham is a fantastic game, so we completely understand why you’d love to see it on All On Board! We regularly gather feedback and game requests from the community, and suggestions like yours are always valuable to us. We share news about upcoming content and plans on our Discord roadmap (https://discord.gg/EumRYpZ3YW), so feel free to join us there and keep an eye on future updates. Thanks again for taking the time to share your thoughts and for supporting All On Board!

reviews.review_description:
I finally decided to give this title a try after much back-and-forth over the last few months. I saw th￼e Devs had recently completed an update with some improvements that I thought might finally make it worth my while. 

To be clear, I do not play board games on a regular basis in real life. But I enjoy some of the classics and would love to explore more board games in the VR medium. I think VR is a great place for board games and tabletop experiences and I wish we had more of this type of thing. 

The immersive environments that are designed for each game looked very, very cool and engaging. But when I started playing, ￼ I realized that the tabletop simulator part of this meant absolutely no game mechanics to help you progress through the game, or know when something is being done right or wrong, or provide any additional engagement. 

The game previews￼ are a great idea and ￼I watched one of the tutorial videos for the Dark Castle game to give it a try, but I still didn’t get a clear sense of the gameplay from that video alone. It still seemed confusing and hard to get into with so many things to remember as a new player.

This game badly needs mechanics. I think a medium like VR should enhance existing real life experiences. But in order for this game to be a success, this bare bones approach to mechanics has to change. The average person is not going to take the time to read through an entire Wiki on a game or watch and re-watch a video to learn how to play. 

And every player would need to do this if they don’t already know how to play the game. I could see if this was Uno or Monopoly or some very well-known boardgames that most people know how to play. But these are niche games for folks who are already into the medium of board gaming. The average person is not going to have heard of these games or have any idea how to facilitate a good gameplay session￼.

I think if the Devs can listen to customer feedback, which has been plenty and forthcoming, and despite their original idea for what the game could be…realize that the public is asking for step-by-step tutorials and game mechanics so that these games are playable for the average person with no experience, then they can have a successful game. 

If they stick with their hardheaded approach to this being a tabletop simulator and nothing else, ￼then this will continue to be a title that gets buried on the store with potential and customers lost.￼ I originally wanted to buy this to try it so that my SO could also buy it and we could play together but, ￼unfortunately it’s a refund for me.

developer_response.body: Thank you for taking the time to write such a detailed review. We’re sorry to hear that the experience didn’t match what you expected, and we sincerely appreciate how clearly you explained your perspective. We understand your concerns. Our initial goal with All On Board! was to recreate the feeling of sitting around a real table, although we know this approach can feel overwhelming for players discovering these board games for the first time. Everything you’ve mentioned is completely fair. We’ve been working to make the learning curve smoother. A new onboarding experience is already available to help new players feel more comfortable from the start. All of our current games also include their full rulebooks inside the app, so instructions are always within reach. We’re also expanding our accessibility and guidance tools. Smart Glance was just an early step, and we’re committed to improving in-app support so that new players don’t feel lost when they begin. Our goal is for anyone, no matter their background or experience with board games, to enjoy the platform confidently. Thank you again for giving All On Board! a try. Feedback like yours helps us understand where things can improve and guides how the platform evolves.

reviews.review_description:
Right. Just do not listen to the negative reviews by people who simply have not understood what this is.
This is like Tabletop Simulator, which I have played in VR, and we had lots of issues with moving other's game components whenever we moved our own. It was unplayable. 
But this game works perfectly. 
The graphics are excellent. I would recommend Quest Games Optimiser to increase resolution, but it is certainly not required. 
The game boards and assets are extremely high quality. Manipulating items and dice is simple and intuitive.
Repositioning yourself is easy.
AND, although there aren't rule books for each game, there are in-game videos that teach each game perfectly. If you struggle with any rules, just ask ChatGPT - it knows all of the rules.
The surrounding environments change for each game. For example, Escape the Dark Castle places your game table in a dungeon. 
The DLC games are great for initial choices. Terraforming Mars - great game. Escape the Dark Castle - great game. Istanbul - great game. Rallyman- great game. And more - and clearly more to come.
Devs, thanks for this game, and thanks for allowing other players to play the DLC games as long as one person has bought it. I bought all DLC immediately and gifted this game to three of my mates. Worth every penny.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your amazing review! Reading your message truly made our day. We’re incredibly grateful for your support, your thoughtful feedback, and your enthusiasm for the platform. It’s so great to hear that you and your friends are enjoying the experience, and we deeply appreciate you spreading the love by gifting the platform. We’re continuing to improve and expand All On Board!, and it’s thanks to players like you that we’re able to keep moving forward with confidence. Hope to keep surprising you with more to come!

reviews.review_description: I get the idea, but only after purchasing. I went gungho in and bought the mega download pack, and I was expecting some computer interaction.  However, I think all you get is a virtual board game with all the pieces. No computer ai to play against, so if no one is online, you can't play. Games that feature solo games again, you're doing all the movement as you would in your own world. I was expecting prompts, but all I got from 40 quid is 5 virtual board games.

developer_response.body: Thanks for taking the time to share your thoughts. All On Board! is a social VR platform where players gather around a virtual table to move pieces manually and interact freely, much like they would in a physical board game session. It focuses on shared play, whether with friends, in the public lobby, or through community-hosted games. Thanks again for your message. If you're interested, we're happy to share tips on finding active players or making the most of solo setups. You can reach us at support@thegamekitchen.com or through our social and community channels.

reviews.review_description:
Does this mean hero quest is coming 🤔


After owning this game for months and never playing it because I thought a plethora of table top games was going to be coming through here and still no new games in sight its very disappointing I think the whole concept of doing this in vr is great though

developer_response.body: Thank you for your review. New titles are on the way, carefully prepared to ensure they meet the quality this platform aims for. Great to hear you appreciate the concept! Hopefully, all the upcoming content will change your experience for the better. You might feel like updating your review sooner than expected!

reviews.review_description: Literally the worst VR game ever. I get the “table top simulator” thing but seriously? No menus. No guides.  No rule enforcement mechanisms. You have to keep track of everything yourself. They literally made the environment and physics and went “welp my job here is done”.  This could have been done in meta world horizons as just some random chess board hanging out on a table somewhere. They really took the “simulator” part quite literally. Then the money thing… I paid $10 for chess, checkers and two other basic games. The other games you have to pay for if you want to play. They say you can join other people’s games but I’m pretty sure most people got this to play with their friends already. This seems to walk the line of illegal misleading advertising IMO. Anyone who says this is a value deal is a paid review or a bot. Run

developer_response.body: Thank you for taking the time to share your thoughts. We’re genuinely sorry to hear that All On Board! didn’t meet your expectations. We understand that a more guided experience might have been expected. All On Board! aims to capture the feel of real-life tabletop gaming, where players manage the rules themselves. While this approach isn’t for everyone, we’ve included features like video tutorials and Smart Glance, which helps you quickly identify and understand each game component at a quick look. We’re also continuing to improve onboarding and usability based on player feedback. As for pricing and content, the base version of All On Board! includes four classic games. As noted in the platform description, additional DLC titles are available for purchase. That said, we are currently reviewing all related materials to ensure there is no room for confusion. With the base game, players can also try out all available DLCs in sandbox mode, and every week we highlight a Featured Game of the Week that is fully unlocked and playable for a limited time. One of the platform’s key strengths is that only one person needs to own a DLC to host a game. Friends can join and play without having to purchase it themselves. We warmly invite you to join our Discord server, where you will find other players to connect with and enjoy the platform together. We really appreciate your feedback. It helps us continue shaping All On Board! into something the community values.

reviews.review_description: Don't know what people were expecting for the price point but I think the value here is amazing, you can play games as long as someone owns them but for me even the value of buying the games individually is well worth it ! Much cheaper than buying them physically, would love to see some features like perhaps a practice ai for solo players , even if very limited, would also love an actual rulebook in the game (unless it's already in there and I just can't find it) the video tutorials are super useful and very helpful when starting out but a manual is sometimes just more practical when actually playing or just to check certain sections

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot for your thoughtful review. We're really happy to hear that you're finding good value in the platform and enjoying the flexibility of being able to play any game as long as someone in the group owns it. It's great to know the video tutorials helped you get started, and we appreciate your suggestions about solo play options and having an in-game rulebook. Feedback like yours really helps us understand how players are using the platform and what could make the experience even better. Thanks again for your support and for being part of the journey!

reviews.review_title: I can see the potential, but…
reviews.review_description: I was super excited for this release and I bought 4 of the DLC on release. Unfortunately the game seems to expect you to know all the nuances and rules of the games you purchase without providing a rulebook. Sure there is a “tutorial video.” But they are bare bones at best.  Your best bet is to search YouTube and watch people actually playing the game to get a better sense of what to do. The custom backgrounds for the games are quite nice and I can tell a lot of love went into this but seeing how they have the rights to the games and all of the pieces I’m absolutely stumped as to why there is no rulebook, what could be argued is the most essential piece of a board game.  Maybe it’s in there and I’m just missing something. Devs please get back to me on this as I really want this to be a 5 star game.

developer_response.body:
Hi! Thank you for your feedback! In All on Board!, we strive to recreate the tabletop experience as closely as possible to real life, so players need to know the rules and apply them during the game. We've put a lot of effort into making our video tutorials easy to understand, but we know that the same information doesn't work the same for everyone. This is where your feedback is invaluable, helping us identify areas for improvement.

We are fully committed to enhancing the learning experience for each tabletop game, and we're already working on it. This is just the beginning, and we have much more to offer. Your support is very important to us!

reviews.review_description:
I've been waiting years for something like this to come along. The MR experience is awesome. Glad to see they're doing the Walkabout Mini Golf buisness model.
Really hope for mod support.

3/4/2025 Additional comment, some scripted actions should be added to make gameplay more fluid and would make this easier for most users.

Higher rendering resolution and removing fixed foveated rendering for Quest 3 users is a must!

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for your kind words! We're really glad to hear you're enjoying the MR experience and that the platform delivered what you’d been waiting for. We really appreciate your feedback, and it’s always helpful to hear how different players approach the platform. We'll keep paying close attention to what the community shares as we continue shaping what's next. Thanks again for your support!

reviews.review_description: I love board games and card games. I'm a social gamer. so I had to buy this app as soon as it came out. The game play and setup are very smooth. Everything seems to work as it should. It comes with 5 free classic games, chess (chess is missing a chess clock, though). Checkers, Parcheesi, Ludo (Ludo can be accessed when you select parcheesi) , GO, these free games don't have  too many scripts on them, I like it, it means you can make some house rules on the fly. The premium games look good but the only game I was familiar with was Terraforming Mars, I've never been interested in that game though, but I know it's very popular in the board game world, so good job on the devs for getting that game; along with the other premium games. The Premium games have some scripting on them to help with the complex rules of those games to avoid a lot of crunch. I bought Escape the Dark Castle, I haven't played it with a group yet, but it looks promising to me. This app has a lot of potential. Hopefully, they can add more premium games. I'd like to have a deck of cards added to the free game list so we could play card games, create our own card games, or house rules for known card games. I really like the unscripted sandbox we get with the free games.

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot for your thoughtful review! We're glad to hear you've been enjoying the platform and that the experience felt smooth and flexible. It's great to know the free classic games and their more open setup have worked well for your playstyle. We also appreciate your thoughts on the premium games and the scripting support they include. Your suggestions around card-based options and house rules are really interesting, and they help us understand the kind of experiences players value most. Thanks again for your support and for being part of the community as All On Board! continues to grow.

reviews.review_description: I've been waiting for a platform like table top simulator or tabletop playground to make it to standalone devices. With the resurgence of board games it only makes sense. Being able to talk and play with other thousands of miles away  just like they are in the same room as amazing. While I would like the games to be narrated and completely scripted, being forced to actually learn how to play and teaching others adds to the fun

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for your kind words! We're thrilled to hear that the platform is delivering the kind of VR experience you were hoping for, especially when it comes to bringing people together across long distances. It's also great to know you’ve found joy not only in the games themselves but in learning and sharing how to play them. That spirit of connection and discovery is exactly what we had in mind, and we’re so glad to have you as part of the community.

reviews.review_title: I feel like I got ripped off
reviews.review_description:
I paid 10 bucks for checkers, I am very mad about it very misleading, I hope people see my review and understand literally you pay 10 bucks for checkers then you have to buy other board games, and for me I never heard of these board games they have for sell.

I think I got ripped off

developer_response.body:
Hi,

Thank you for your feedback! We’re truly sorry to hear that your experience hasn’t been as positive as we’d hoped. We understand your frustration, and we genuinely care about addressing your concerns.

The base game includes 4 classic games. We are working on expanding this collection in the future. Purchasing DLC is only required to host games, you can always join games of any title using just the base game. Our catalog currently consists of 6 acclaimed titles (all of them with outstanding reviews in BGG!) with many more to come via frequent updates.

reviews.review_description:
I'm excited this is finally here. I think people upset about the price are just unfamiliar with how expensive tabletop gaming can be. This is, by far, cheaper. 

My criticism is that there needs to be actual tutorials, and the rulebook should be available. I hadn't played Terraforming Mars for quite some time, so I basically need to learn it all over again. The video doesn't really help much. At least, not for me. A step-by-step tutorial game would be ideal, but even just adding the rulebook would solve the problem

developer_response.body:
Hi! Thanks for your feedback! You've made a good point about the pricing. We wanted to make All on Board! as accessible as possible, and the price was an important factor. That's why we also decided that only the host (the person who creates the game session) needs to own the DLC. This way, it's affordable, and you can join any game session for free.

Regarding video tutorials, we are aware that some people are struggling, and we are working on it. In the meantime, we recommend engaging with other players in our Discord server or checking out other resources to help you get up and running to enjoy All on Board!.

We are fully committed to improving the experience.

reviews.review_title: Good start point if you have friends
reviews.review_description:
I haven't bought any dlc yet. So far the game feels good.  The ambience and music is good. The graphics are good. The free games are good but...

Is made to play with friends. I know in the future they will expand to flat screen users like phones so I can play with a friend who play in a tablet or phone even if they don't have VR. However, in this AI world having an AI opponent to play whenever you have time, without worrying about leaving the match and continue it later on I think is basic. If I want to play chess I have to play myself alone for both players, same for Parchese or the other free games. It makes me feel kinda depressed, like being alone was not enough I have to play with myself and move myself both sides. 
Another thing it would be nice to have like a glass to put the 🎲 inside and move the glass and throw both 🎲 at the same time. 

I hope you can invest a little bit on this things specially the AI to give everyone the opportunity to play with freedom of time and without thinking if you have to go without disturbing other's game.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for your thoughtful review. We're glad to hear you're enjoying the visual atmosphere and overall feel of the platform. All On Board! is designed to recreate the experience of sitting around a real table with others, which is why it's centered on real-time multiplayer. We really appreciate hearing different perspectives on how people like to enjoy their games, and your message helps us better understand the variety of playstyles out there. Thanks again for sharing your impressions and for being part of the community.

reviews.review_title: Nice to have a Quest Tabletop game
reviews.review_description:
I've not played much yet, I love board games and so far I do have some feedback from the little I played.

It would be nice to see some sort of AI opponent added, I'm a pretty introverted person and only play with a select few set of friends, so having some AI opponent to play games against would be great. (if there is an AI option please do let me know I didn't see a setting for it, the Dummies didn't do anything for me)

I'd also like to have the option to pick up multiple dice at the same time, picking up one dice at a time is finicky and immersion-breaking for me. 

I don't have much time in the game yet so it's possible that I haven't found the option for these things yet, if so please let me know 😅 but for now I'm giving it 3 stars cause I'm really missing these features, AI and picking up multiple dice.

I will update this review if I find these features.

Little update!:
I played a little more last day and found out that you can pick up multiple dice at the same time, it's just not that intuitive in my opinion, what you need to is pick up the first dice with Trigger if I remember correctly then when you point / select another dice, you can pick it up with the Grip button, but to me it felt like it didn't always look,  I also clicked a few time on the B button which appeared to change something about the dice pick up but I'm not so sure.

developer_response.body:
Hi! Thank you for sharing your thoughts with us. Let's see if we can help you and get those 5 stars :)

All on Board! is a tabletop simulator for VR, so it tries to reproduce the real thing. That's why players need to learn the rules and find other players to play with. Being introverted is nothing bad at all, and in All on Board!, you can set up a game by choosing some tags to better define the mood and tone you're more comfortable with. Then, in our Discord server, you'll find the LFG channels, where you can interact with other people to share your session code or join an existing match that meets your preferences, for instance: no mic and relaxed.

Bear in mind that All on Board! has just launched, so more people will be joining, creating more opportunities to play with strangers or friends.

reviews.review_title: Boards are cheaper than physical copies
reviews.review_description: People should have expected to be charged more for better board games, the physical copies are not free so why would they be on a 10 dollar game. You get what you pay for here.

reviews.review_title: I have been waiting for this!!!
reviews.review_description:
Would love to give 5 stars but:

1. The games do not guide you through them like you'd expect.

2. There are no rules.

3. It's just the pieces brought into the game, you have to know how to play the games already in order to play.

4. Grabbing things is overly complicated.

5. There is no lobby or list of open games hosted by others that you can join.

6. There are no AI's to play against.

7. There's no way to hold cards in your hand that I can figure out. 

8. It's too easy to accidentally grab something random off the board and then you have to remember where that piece was to put it back.

9. Cards drop from your hand way too easily.

I really don't understand why the games don't walk you through them, like Catan VR does, or even like Board Game Arena or any app for any boardgame I've ever played. This app is great for folks who already know and love the games, but for those of us who aren't sure of the rules, it's impossible.

developer_response.body: Thanks for taking the time to write such a thoughtful review. It's clear you gave the platform a solid try, and we really appreciate the way you've described what worked for you and what didn’t. Every bit of feedback like this helps us understand how people are actually experiencing the platform. Thanks again for jumping in and for sharing your take.

reviews.review_description:
The games don't come with rule books, which means you can only play them if you know the rules. The game doesn't know the rules either, it just basically simulates the physical game and the players need to enforce the rules just like IRL, except you haven't got a rule book. I tried Dark Castle and I can't even figure out how to start a game. You get a video but that's not great for learning how to play. 
The other problem is that there are not many people playing this, so finding players online is basically hopeless and you need an established group of friends to play this.

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot for your review! We appreciate you taking the time to share your thoughts. All On Board! is built to offer a flexible and social experience that feels close to playing around a real table, where players manage the game flow themselves. We’ve taken note of your feedback regarding the tutorial format, and we’re always paying attention to how players are approaching the learning process. If you’re looking to join others, our community channels (like Discord) are available and might help you find people to play with. Thanks again for being part of this.

reviews.review_description: I played my first full game using the dlc, Istanbul and thought it played pretty well.  I’ve played this game in person and very much enjoyed playing it in VR.  I’m  looking forward to more dlc title releases. The game is a great value as these dlc’s, if bought in person, are quite expensive.  A suggestion for the developers would be an addition to the game tutorial.  I spent quite a bit of time trying to figure out how to use the right combination of buttons on my controller to pick up and drop the assistants.  I still haven’t been able to move the cube on the mail place after taking items from there; and how to toss the dice with one hand.  Maybe a picture of the controllers with directions and arrows could improve the learning curve. I highly recommend “All On Board” and look forward to what’s next. Thanks to the developers for bringing board gaming to VR.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your kind words!

Rest assured that the team continues to work hard to improve the platform. This is just the beginning!

We also have a suggestions channel you can use, on our Discord server: https://discord.gg/CyUFWpWSny

reviews.review_description: This was a trap I feel like.. The games that our given with the game I can get for free on multiple free apps on my quest3. Then the games that might be good your telling me I have to pay for. Very disappointing. I was so excited maybe you should remake your video saying oh yea these games you see n video our sold separately...  con artisits

developer_response.body: Hi! We're really sorry that you felt disappointed, and we appreciate your feedback. We'll make sure to improve the platform's description to avoid any misleading information. The base platform includes classic games like Chess, Checkers, Ludo, and Go, while the licensed games are sold separately as DLC. In All on Board!, if you own a DLC, you can invite other players to join your session, even if they don't own that DLC. Please email us at support@thegamekitchen.com for personalized support.

reviews.review_description:
Love it. Couple minor issues. There are times when it’s meant to be intuitive and it isn’t. Like picking up a card in escape the castle, one type is trigger the other is use the A or X. 

Instructions — maybe I missed it but I wish I could have full readable instructions somewhere within the game. Watching and skimming the YouTube is ok but not as fast as looking at the manual when you’re learning the game. 

Really enjoying it and am hoping they publish more games or even take requests for games somewhere.

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot for your thoughtful review! We're really glad to hear you're enjoying the game. We appreciate your feedback regarding controls and instructions. Comments like these help us better understand how players experience the platform in practice. It’s always helpful to hear what players find most useful when learning the game. Thanks again for playing and for taking the time to share your thoughts!

reviews.review_description:
Bought the game and Escape the castle! Jumped on with another dude and neither of us had a clue at first but we both had an absolute blast! 

Can't decide what game to purchase next, torn between Binding of Isaac and Teraphorming Mars

Could the devs possibly settle this for me? 😂

developer_response.body:
Thanks for your kind words!

Choosing the game depends more on your personal taste, as both are great games. Let's try to help: Terraforming Mars and The Binding of Isaac: Four Souls are both considered strategy games. The former is considered harder to learn and longer to play, while the latter is of normal difficulty and shorter to play. So it depends on the game experience you expect, or maybe the mood you are in before you play. Hope that helps!

reviews.review_description: They have a nice selection of board games for almost everyone at launch.  They were reasonably priced so I picked them all up to try. I can’t wait to see what’s on the horizon for more games. Thanks Devs!

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for your kind words! We’re thrilled to hear that you’ve been enjoying the game selection at launch and that you found them reasonably priced. It’s great to know you’ve already picked them all up to try, and we’re just as excited as you are about what’s coming next. Your support means a lot. Thanks again for being part of this!

reviews.review_description: I bought the app and my first board game, The Binding of Issac.  Immediately hosted a match and met several great people and played a round of the game.  Had a lot of fun, great start and looking forward to the future

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for your kind words! We're really glad to hear you had such a great first experience with All On Board!, from setting up a match to meeting awesome people. It’s always encouraging to know that the platform helps bring players together and makes game nights feel fun and easy. We’re excited to keep growing and hope you'll continue enjoying everything that’s coming next. Thanks again for being part of the ride!

reviews.review_description: You pay for the game. And you have to pay for the games inside the game. Not for me I'm not Mr money bags.

developer_response.body:
Hi,

Thank you for your feedback! We’re truly sorry to hear that your experience hasn’t been as positive as we’d hoped. We understand your frustration, and we genuinely care about addressing your concerns.

The base game includes 4 classic games. We are working on expanding this collection in the future. Purchasing DLC is only required to host games, you can always join games of any title using just the base game. 

We are reviwing the information on the store page to better reflect this.

reviews.review_description: plz

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot for your message. We really appreciate you taking the time to share your interest in Korean language support. Feedback like this helps us understand what players from different regions would like to see.

reviews.review_description: Will update review if things get better.

developer_response.body:
Hi! We take very seriously our player's feedback in order to keep improving the platform and game's experience. Don't hesitate to share yours. You can do it in the #support channel in our Discord server or by email to support@thegamekitchen.com
Thanks!

reviews.review_description: This right here is the reason I bought a VR headset in the first place a year ago. I just bought the game and haven't played yet. I just wanted to pop in and say thank you to the developers. Since I have to give a star rating, I'll start with 4. I'll be back after I play to edit.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for your message! It means a lot to hear that All On Board! was one of the reasons you got into VR in the first place. We’re really glad you took the time to share your appreciation, and we hope you enjoy your first games when you get the chance to dive in. Looking forward to hearing your thoughts once you've had a chance to play!

reviews.review_description: Works really well

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot for the kind words! We're really glad to hear everything’s working smoothly and that you're enjoying the experience. It means a lot to see feedback like this from players who are having a good time with the platform.
